title: Brothers, town employees, allegedly attacked homeless man in Holbrook and broke several of his bones
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brothers Jesse and Jake Baxter are charged with assaulting a homeless man in Holbrook, breaking several bones.
The attack allegedly took place near Brookville Park on June 13; the victim was mistaken for another man.
Holbrook Police leveraged vehicle data and cameras to implicate the Baxters in the felony assault charges.

title: ‘It’s about my life’: Why acclaimed Cambridge wine bar Dear Annie is closing
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Dear Annie wine bar in Cambridge will close its dining room on July 31 to prioritize owner's family life.
Owner Lauren Friel plans to reopen in September with a focus on private and bespoke wine events.
Dear Annie, opened in 2021, gained national acclaim, appearing on lists by The New York Times and Bon Appétit.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ 2026 Summer League roster and schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics' Summer League roster features Hugo Gonzalez and first-round pick Chris Cenac Jr.
Celtics' Summer League games scheduled from July 10-15, all held at Cox Pavilion.
Amile Jefferson serves as head coach for the Celtics' 2026 Summer League team.

title: Tartan Army makes a big donation to help Mass. students in need
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Tartan Army donated $15,000 to the Massachusetts Child charity, benefiting students in need.
The donation resulted from savings by using school buses for transportation to World Cup matches in Foxborough.
The funds will assist Massachusetts students with economic hardships in purchasing clothing, books, and school supplies.

title: Your neighborhood Cumbies could soon look a little different
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cumberland Farms' parent company, EG Group, filed to go public, planning renovations and expansions nationwide.
The chain aims to refresh 60 Cumberland Farms stores annually, targeting 600 sites primarily in the Northeast.
Cumberland rebranded numerous stores; plans include introducing Krispy Krunch Chicken in 40 Loaf 'N Jug locations.

title: Boston sues social media companies over ‘addictive’ features, joining nationwide litigation
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu announced a lawsuit against Meta, TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube targeting addictive features aimed at minors.
The suit will be consolidated with over 1,500 similar cases, seeking removal of features and compensation for mental health support.
Between 2015 and 2021, reports of persistent sadness among Boston high school students rose from 26.7% to 43.9%.

title: Cyclospora, the explosive diarrhea parasite, has reached Mass. Here’s what to know.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts has reported 18 cases of cyclosporiasis since May 1, according to the state's Department of Public Health.
Cyclospora, a parasite causing diarrhea, spreads through contaminated food or water and has led to 145 reported cases nationwide.
Previous U.S. outbreaks linked to fresh produce like raspberries, basil, and scallions; washing can remove but cooking kills the parasite.

title: Jaylen Brown says he and Patriots WR A.J. Brown are cousins
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown revealed he and Patriots receiver A.J. Brown are cousins, sharing grandfathers who are brothers.
Both Jaylen and A.J. Brown, age 29, have won championships with different teams in 2024.
Jaylen was traded from the Celtics to the 76ers; A.J. moved from the Eagles to the Patriots in recent trades.

title: Carles Gil selected for 2026 MLS All-Star roster
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Carles Gil was selected for the MLS All-Star Game, marking his fifth All-Star appearance.
The game will take place on July 29 at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte.
Gil is the only Revolution player on the 29-member roster, named as a coach's selection.

title: Massachusetts man who fell ill in Fiji dies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Scott 'Scooter' Winslow from Holbrook died after falling critically ill in Fiji during a trip that started in April.
Winslow developed a critical infection, possibly from an insect bite, and was admitted to an ICU in a local hospital.
A request to medflight Winslow to Mass General Hospital was denied, but his daughters managed to visit him before he passed away.
